description: Canadian organized crime group based in Montreal, Quebec, originating from the Irish-Canadian enclave of Pointe-Saint-Charles in the 1950s. Initially involved in truck hijackings, bank robberies, and extortion, it later moved into drug trafficking, controlling illegal activity at the Port of Montreal. The gang formed a partnership with the Cali Cartel under Allan Ross and remains active with an estimated 125-150 members.

country_links.evidence_quote: an officer with the Service de police de la Ville de Montréal on vacation in Playa del Carmen in Mexico saw two other officers... socializing with members of the West End Gang and the Montreal Hells Angels at the Blue Parrot nightclub... the Hells Angels and the West End Gang members proceed to beat the policeman bloody
country_links.context: Members of the West End Gang socialized with police and Hells Angels in Mexico and assaulted a policeman there.

country_links.evidence_quote: shipments of up to 200 kilograms being sent to the Netherlands
country_links.context: The West End Gang sent cocaine shipments to the Netherlands.

country_links.evidence_quote: shipments of up to 200 kilograms being sent to the Netherlands and the United Kingdom
country_links.context: The West End Gang sent cocaine shipments to the United Kingdom.

country_links.evidence_quote: Territory: Primarily Greater Montreal, with additional territory throughout Quebec, as well as South Florida
country_links.context: The West End Gang has territory in South Florida.
